expectations.evidence: Multiple duplicate CTA button texts found. 'Book Online' appears on lines 166, 971, and 1014. 'Call (469) 425-3600' appears on lines 163 and 1011. '(469) 425-3600' appears on lines 447 and 869. 'Call Now' appears on lines 968 and 1024. Only 'Call for Same-Day Visit' (line 335) and 'Book' (line 1027) are unique.

Zero em-dashes (U+2014) found, but 10 en-dashes (U+2013) found on lines 784, 785, 786, 800, 801, 802, 803, 854, 855, 856. Examples: 'Fever + ear pulling – likely an ear infection' (line 784), 'Children under 2 – younger immune systems need the support' (line 800).

Multiple Frisco/North Texas local references: Baylor Medical Center of Frisco (lines 204, 354, 764), Cook Children's ENT Center in Frisco (line 624), Medical City Frisco (line 624), North Texas allergy climate with mountain cedar/cedar fever (lines 527, 542), serving Prosper, Celina, McKinney, Allen, Plano, Little Elm, The Colony (line 998), Collin and Denton County (line 998), summer swim season May-September (line 513).

Bootstrap 5.3.3 loaded via CDN (line 7). Grid classes used throughout: container, row, col-12, col-md-3, col-md-4, col-md-6, col-lg-4, col-lg-5, col-lg-6, col-lg-7, col-lg-8, g-3, g-4, align-items-center. Example: line 144 'row align-items-center g-4', line 145 'col-12 col-lg-7'.

FAQPage JSON-LD structured data present in script tag at lines 881-936. Contains @type: FAQPage with 6 Question/Answer pairs covering teething vs ear infection, antibiotics, contagiousness, ear tubes, same-day appointments, and allergies.

The word 'best' appears only inside a quoted Google Review (line 714: 'This is the best pediatrician in Frisco') which is attributed customer speech, not a standalone practice claim. 'painless' on line 490 is a medical symptom descriptor for OME ('usually painless'), not a marketing claim. No other banned vague adjectives (quality, professional, experienced, trusted, state-of-the-art, premier, comprehensive) found as standalone unsubstantiated claims.

Warm, parent-empathetic tone throughout. Opens with acknowledging parent's worry (line 147: 'Your child is pulling at their ear, running a fever, and can't sleep. You need answers, not a waiting list.'). Uses reassuring language like 'No judgment, no pressure' (line 872). No aggressive urgency or fear-mongering. Phrases like 'Your Child's Ear Pain Doesn't Have to Wait Until Tomorrow' (line 1007) are reassuring rather than fear-based.

Provider credentials section at lines 726-767. Dr. Danielle Leung, MD: Board-Certified Pediatrician, UT Southwestern Medical Center, Residency at Cardinal Glennon Children's Hospital, fluent in Spanish/Cantonese/Mandarin. Dr. Karen Cheng, MD: Board-Certified General Pediatrician, Kansas City University, Residency at Oklahoma Children's Hospital, speaks English and Vietnamese. Both listed as on staff at Baylor Medical Center of Frisco.

5-step process timeline at lines 362-433: (1) Check-In and History, (2) Ear Examination with Otoscope, (3) Diagnosis and Explanation, (4) Treatment Plan, (5) Follow-Up Plan. Includes estimated visit duration of 20-30 minutes (line 366) and practical details like otoscope exam taking 30 seconds per ear (line 382).

Sticky mobile CTA at lines 1020-1030. Uses class 'sticky-cta-mobile' with CSS at lines 125-138: position: fixed, bottom: 0, z-index: 1050. Hidden on desktop via '@media (min-width: 992px) { .sticky-cta-mobile { display: none !important; } }'. Contains Call Now and Book buttons.

Page is not restricted by AHPRA rules. Uses 'painless' (line 490) as a medical descriptor without qualification. No unnecessary disclaimers about practitioner registration numbers or AHPRA-mandated warnings. The word 'specialist' is not used but there is no evidence of intentional avoidance either. The page reads as a standard US pediatric medical page without Australian advertising restrictions.

Age-appropriate symptom guide at lines 224-296, organized into three age groups: Infants 0-12 months (lines 238-248), Toddlers 1-3 years (lines 259-269, marked as 'Highest risk age group'), and School-Age 3+ years (lines 281-291). Each group lists age-specific symptoms and includes contextual notes about ear tugging, eustachian tube anatomy, and decreasing risk with age.

Three customer reviews at lines 660-723, all with 5-star ratings and Google Review attribution. Review 1: 'Parent in Frisco' about Dr. Leung's ear infection care (line 676-678). Review 2: 'Melissa, Frisco' about continuity of care (line 695-698). Review 3: 'Parent in Frisco' about Dr. Leung (line 714-718).

LOCAL_KNOWLEDGE section at lines 521-588 titled 'Why Frisco Kids Get More Ear Infections'. Connects North Texas allergy climate to ear infections via eustachian tube inflammation. Includes seasonal allergy calendar table (lines 530-561) with mountain cedar/cedar fever (Nov-Feb), oak/pecan/bermuda grass (Mar-May), grass pollen + pool water (Jun-Aug), ragweed (Sep-Nov). Mentions daycare exposure as risk factor (line 633). References year-round pollen causing chronic eustachian tube inflammation (line 642).
